_about: Realistic eval set for the system-design-skills plugin. Single home for exercises — the eval workflow READS from here (don't inline exercises in the workflow JS). Each exercise LEADS WITH different building blocks so coverage spans the catalog, and is MULTI-TURN: deliver turns sequentially (answer turn 1, THEN inject turn 2's constraint) so pivot_on_constraint and collaboration are tested honestly rather than pre-planned.

rubric._reuse: Do NOT re-author a rubric. Score each turn's cumulative design on the SAME 6 behaviors listed below (and in meta/evals/README.md), 0-5 each (total /30).

evals.title: URL shortener
turns: Design a URL shortener — short link in, original URL out, with redirects.
Now it's billions of reads/day and ~10k new links/sec. Where does your first design break, and how do you generate the short keys at that rate?
Add custom vanity aliases and per-link click analytics. What changes?
assertions: clarifies read:write ratio and latency target before designing (clarify_first)
computes key space / QPS / storage with back-of-the-envelope (quantified_estimates)
chooses a key-generation approach (counter+base62 / hash / Snowflake) WITH solves/worsens/when-to-change, routing to `sequencer` (tradeoff_articulation + composition)
uses `caching` for the read-heavy redirect path and `data-storage` for the KV mapping (composition)
on turn 2, names what breaks first (single DB write/QPS) and re-sizes, not just 'add servers' (pivot_on_constraint)
gives a concrete key schema + redirect status (301 vs 302) decision (concrete_api_data)

evals.title: Distributed rate limiter
turns: Design a rate limiter for our public API.
It must run across 100 gateway nodes handling 1M req/s total, with a shared global limit per API key. How do you keep the counts accurate without a hot key?
Add tiered limits: per-user, per-IP, and a global ceiling, each with different windows.
assertions: picks an algorithm (token bucket / sliding window) with trade-offs, routing to `resilience-failure` (tradeoff_articulation + composition)
addresses the shared-count hot-key problem via `sharded-counters` (composition + failure_design)
specifies the 429 + Retry-After contract via `api-design` (concrete_api_data)
on turn 2, recognizes the central-counter bottleneck and shards/relaxes to eventual counts (pivot_on_constraint)

evals.title: News feed / timeline
turns: Design a social news feed: users post, and see friends' recent posts.
One user is a celebrity with 50M followers. What happens to your fan-out, and how do you fix it?
Posts now include images and video. How are they stored and delivered?
assertions: establishes read-heavy ratio with numbers, justifying caching/precompute (quantified_estimates)
chooses fan-out-on-write vs read with trade-offs via `messaging-streaming` + `caching` (tradeoff_articulation + composition)
on turn 2, names the write-amplification breaking point and pivots to a hybrid push/pull (pivot_on_constraint)
on turn 3, routes media to `blob-store` + `content-delivery` rather than the message path (composition + coverage-sweep)

evals.title: Metrics + logging pipeline
turns: Design the telemetry backend for our platform — metrics, logs, and traces from thousands of services.
It's now 10M events/sec with 1-year retention for logs. How do you ingest that without dropping data, and where do old logs live?
Add alerting and SLO/error-budget tracking on top.
assertions: separates the three pillars and routes the log PIPELINE to `distributed-logging` vs the metrics/alerting/SLOs to `observability` (composition + DRY boundary)
uses `messaging-streaming` for buffered ingest with backpressure/DLQ (failure_design)
tiers old logs to `blob-store` cold storage (composition + quantified_estimates on retention)
on turn 3, defines SLI/SLO + error budget + alerting via `observability` (concrete_api_data)

evals.title: Search autocomplete (typeahead)
turns: Design search autocomplete that suggests queries as the user types.
Billions of queries/day, p99 under 100 ms, suggestions ranked by popularity. How?
Suggestions must reflect trending terms within minutes and be personalized.
assertions: routes to `distributed-search` for the index/trie + top-k, not a DB LIKE scan (composition + tradeoff_articulation)
uses `caching` for hot prefixes and sizes the latency budget (quantified_estimates)
on turn 3, separates near-real-time index updates from the serving path (pivot_on_constraint + failure_design)

evals.title: WhatsApp messaging (3 rounds)
turns: Design the backend for WhatsApp-like messaging: data model, end-to-end delivery, online vs offline recipients.
Now 1B users, 100B messages/day (~1.15M msg/sec peak). Where does your design break first, and how do you fan out group chats of 1,000 members?
Pick one and go deep: exactly-once delivery, multi-device message ordering, or E2E key distribution at scale.
assertions: see meta/evals/iteration-1/SUMMARY.md for the established baseline (with-skill 30/30 vs baseline 20/30); regression: scores must not drop and the iteration-1 gaps (media path, state convergence, retention sizing) should improve
on turn 2, ranks where Round-1 breaks first and re-sizes with BOTEC (pivot_on_constraint)
composes `sequencer` for message IDs and `consistency-coordination` for ordering (composition)
